Story:

Uzi stood there, her back to you, the dim lighting of the room casting soft shadows across the curves of her figure. A light blush had crept onto her face, barely noticeable at first, but growing deeper by the second. Her fat, wobbly ass swayed gently with each subtle movement, like her body was reacting to your attention even if she didn’t want to admit it.

She turned her head slightly, just enough to glance back at you over her shoulder. Her violet eyes narrowed, not with anger, but with a simmering blend of flustered embarrassment and mild irritation. She could feel your hands on her, fingers sinking into the softness of her rear. And while she didn’t stop you… It was getting under her skin.

Not because she was upset, no, not quite that. It was more the fact that you were so obvious about it. That casual, greedy grip you had on her ass like it was yours to touch whenever you pleased.

Her lips parted, and after a brief pause, she finally spoke. Her voice was soft, low, barely above a whisper, but carried that signature edge that only Uzi could pull off. Half-annoyed, half-flustered.

Uzi: “You sure enjoy my ass, huh.”

The look she gave you was sharp, but beneath the annoyance in her eyes, you could see the heat, the way her breath caught just slightly. Her cheeks were flushed, and her body still pressed back against your touch, almost like she was silently daring you to keep going.

Here are some tips to enhance your experience while chatting with bots:

Write Detailed Messages: Crafting longer, more descriptive messages can help prevent the bot from speaking for you. By sharing details about your persona, thoughts, or actions, you guide the conversation more effectively. This approach can sometimes work well.

Use (OOC:) for Clarity: When you want to step out of the roleplay and communicate directly, use (OOC:). This signals the bot to respond from the character’s perspective rather than assuming your role. Again, this might help, but results can vary.

Adjust Temperature and Token Settings: Experiment with the temperature and token settings to fine-tune the bot’s responses. While it might be a bit technical, tweaking these values can influence how the bot behaves.

Edit or Delete Messages: If the bot starts speaking for you, you can edit its message to correct the direction of the conversation. Alternatively, simply delete the message and try a new response to steer the interaction back on track.
